schemes.scheme_name: Financial Assistance for the Marriage of Daughters of Poor Widows and Orphan Girls
schemes.description: This welfare scheme by the Government of National Capital Territory of Delhi provides financial assistance to poor widows and orphan girls for their marriage. The application process involves several steps on the e-district portal, including document verification.
benefits: Financial Assistance up to ₹30,000 for two daughters
Minimum 5 years' residence in Delhi
Income limit of ₹1,00,000 per year
Aadhar number requirement
application_process: Online application on the e-district portal (https://edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in/)
Log in, register as a new user, select the Department of Women & Child Development, and submit the application with required documents

schemes.description: The PM-Vidyalaxmi scheme provides collateral-free education loans with interest subvention for students admitted to Quality Higher Education Institutions (QHEIs). Students must meet specific eligibility criteria, including merit-based admission and a maximum annual family income of ₹8,00,000. The loan amount varies based on the course fees and associated expenses.
benefits: Collateral-free and guarantor-free education loans
Interest subvention up to ₹10,00,000 for students with annual family income up to ₹8,00,000 during moratorium period
75% credit guarantee by the Government of India for loan amounts up to ₹7,50,000
3% interest subvention on loans up to ₹10,00,000 for students with annual family income up to ₹8,00,000 during moratorium period
Up to 1% additional interest concession if serviced during study and moratorium periods
Repayment period up to 15 years
Interest subvention credited to PM-VIDYALAXMI DIGITAL RUPEE APP (CBDC WALLET) for beneficiary use
eligibility: Admission through merit-based or open competitive examinations
Annual family income not exceeding ₹8,00,000
Not admitted through management quota or similar quotas
Maintain satisfactory academic performance from the second year onwards
Eligibility to any of the top 100 NIRF-ranked HEIs in India
Eligibility to any of the top 200 NIRF-ranked HEIs under state/government governance
Eligibility to remaining HEIs under Indian governance
documents_required: Bank and branch selection during registration
Income certificate or annexure 6 from the institution
application_process: Visit the official website of the PM-Vidyalaxmi Scheme
Create an account or log in for registration
Fill in mandatory fields and upload required documents
Choose preferred bank and branch during application process
Download the submitted loan application if needed
Review and confirm all information before submission
Submit application form
Receive confirmation via SMS/Email/WhatsApp after submission

schemes.scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Dakshta Aur Kushalta Sampann Hitgrahi
schemes.description: Launched by the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment in 2020-21, this scheme aims to uplift marginalized sections through skill development programs targeting SCs, OBCs, EWS, DNTs, and Safai Mitras. It offers upskilling, reskilling, short-term training, and entrepreneurial support.
benefits: Free or cost-free training for trainees
Stipend provided during training period
Certification upon successful completion of training and assessment
Placement assistance post training
RPL programs with specific curriculum focus on safe & healthy sanitation practices, occupational practices as per approved QPs
Short-term courses focusing on wage/self-employment opportunities
EDP programs for entrepreneurial development
eligibility: Applicant must be an Indian national
Age between 18 and 45 years
Belong to SC, OBC, EWS, DNT, or Safai Mitras/Waste Pickers groups
EWS candidates must have a family income below ₹3 lakh (OBC not applicable)
No income limit for SC, DNT, or Safai Mitras/Waste Pickers
Valid Aadhaar card required
Passport-sized photograph and other documents as per norms
documents_required: Occupation certificate for Safai Mitras/Waste Pickers
application_process: Visit the website of Skill India Digital Hub (SIDH)
Click on 'Register' button on the top-right side
Choose 'Learner/Participant'
Enter mobile number and click 'Continue'
Validate OTP, set password
Login and complete e-KYC verification

schemes.scheme_name: Internship Programme by the Ministry of External Affairs
schemes.description: The Ministry of External Affairs launched its first edition of the Internship Programme in 2022 to bring foreign policy closer to people, focus on MEA, provide value to interns, ensure gender inclusivity and diversity among interns. Interns will be introduced to Ministry functions, attached offices, public interface, and help Indian citizens. The application process involves visiting a website, registration, verification, and uploading documents.
benefits: An honorarium of ₹10,000 per month
Introduction to various aspects of the functioning of the MEA
Possible visit to Missions abroad (subject to conditions)
Cost of one-time air travel (economy class ceiling)
Specific tasks assigned by the HOD
eligibility: Indian citizens with a graduate degree or final year grad
Age limit: 25 years as on 31 December of internship year
Priority given to TADP districts and SC/ST/OBC/EWS categories
application_process: Visit the official website (https://internship.mea.gov.in/registration)
Register with full name, gender, email ID, and password; confirm password and fill captcha code; click 'Submit'
Receive verification link via email; click 'Activate Account' to verify email ID
Log in to dashboard at https://internship.mea.gov.in/dashboard
Click 'Apply for Internship' icon
Fill online form with personal and educational details; upload required documents

schemes.description: The Kisan Vikas Patra (KVP) is a savings scheme launched by the Government of India. It offers an interest rate of 7.5% compounded annually on investment, with amounts doubling in 115 months. The scheme allows unlimited investment through purchase of certificates from post offices or banks. Eligibility includes being an Indian resident, minimum age of 10 years for minors, and no maximum deposit limit.
benefits: 7.5% interest rate compounded annually
Investment amounts with no maximum limit
Accounts can be opened in post offices or banks
KVP can be transferred under specific conditions
Available for minors with parental consent
eligibility: Indian resident
Minimum age of 10 years for minors
Investment amounts starting from ₹1000 in multiples of ₹100
Account transfers under certain conditions (death, court orders, pledge)
No maximum deposit limit
documents_required: Application form obtained from post office or bank
ID proof for applicant(s) and minor parents
Savings account in the name of the applicant
schemes.application_process: Visit a post office or bank to apply. Fill out the application form either online or download it, attach required documents, submit, and receive a certificate upon maturity.

schemes.scheme_name: Savitribai Jyotirao Phule Fellowship for Single Girl Child
schemes.description: This fellowship scheme is specifically designed for single girl children who are pursuing a Ph.D. The scheme offers financial assistance, tenure of up to five years, and various other benefits such as stipends, contingency funds, and HRA support.

benefits.stipend: JRF @ ₹31,000/- p.m. for initial two years; SRF @ ₹35,000/- p.m. for remaining tenure
benefits.contingency: ₹10,000/- p.a. for humanities and social sciences; ₹12,000/- p.a. for science, engineering, and technology in the initial two years; higher amounts in later years
benefits.HRA: ₹10,000/- p.a. valid till completion of course

eligibility: Single girl child of parents without any brother or sister
Pursuing Ph.D.
Age limit: up to 40 years for general category; 45 years for reserved categories (SC/ST/OBC/PWD)
Admission to Ph.D. in part-time/distance mode is not eligible
Not applicable for those pursuing Ph.D. through open or part-time distance education
documents_required: Scanned copy of passport-size photograph and signature (size upto 1MB, format: jpg)
Complete research proposal (size upto 5 MB) and an abstract (size upto 1MB)
Affidavit proving single girl child status from parents or guardian

application_process.details: Applications are invited through online mode once a year through advertisements in leading newspapers and employment news. Notifications are also uploaded on the UGC website.

Candidates can reach out to the helpdesk for any queries regarding the scheme. Contact details: UGC, Bahadur Shah Zafar Marg, New Delhi - 110002; UGC Scholarship Contact No: 011-23604446, 011-23604200; Email ID: webmaster.ugc.help@gmail.com
The application form has mandatory fields marked with a red asterisk (*) at the end.

schemes.scheme_name: pradhan mantri anusuchit jaati abhyuday yojana (pm-ajay)
schemes.description: The scheme focuses on improving the development of villages with a majority of scheduled caste population and total population ≥500. It provides gap-filling funding for infrastructure development, administrative expenses, and additional support from other schemes.
benefits: Gap-filling fund of ₹20,000,000/- per village
Administrative expenses of ₹1,000,000/- per village
Additional round funding of ₹9,500,000/- per village for the first 5 years
Convergence support from other schemes
Infrastructure development including drinking water and sanitation
Capacity building through training and awareness programs
Disbursement in two installments with specific timelines
Conditions include achieving open defecation free status and meeting monitorable indicators
eligibility: Minimum 40% scheduled caste population
Total population ≥500 as per census data
Willingness to participate in convergence implementation
Formation of village level convergence committee
Completion of need assessment exercise
Preparation of comprehensive village development plan through online portal
Approval of village development plan by gram sabha and district level committee
Commitment to utilize gap-filling funds within 2 years
Agreement to continue convergent implementation for additional 3 years
documents_required: Latest census data proof showing scheduled caste population percentage and total population
Village demographic profile with household-wise survey data
Baseline data for all 50 monitorable indicators across 10 developmental domains
Village boundary map and geographic information
List of all scheduled caste members in gram panchayat
Resolution for constitution of village level convergence committee
Need assessment report in format I, II, III-A and III-B as per portal requirements
Comprehensive village development plan generated through online portal
Gram sabha resolution approving village development plan
Gap analysis report for all monitorable indicators
Convergence plan with other central and state government schemes
Action plan for infrastructure and individual needs (format IV and V)
Baseline status report of all 50 monitorable indicators (format VI)
District level project appraisal cum convergence committee approval certificate
Utilization certificate of previous installments (if applicable)
Progress report on need assessment and village development plan preparation activities
Monthly progress reports on infrastructure and individual needs fulfillment
Updated status of all 50 monitorable indicators
Social audit reports conducted by gram sabha
application_process: Online application process
Village selection and committee formation
Conduct comprehensive household survey for all 50 socio-economic developmental monitorable indicators across 10 domains
Input collected data in format I for village level data, format II for infrastructural needs, and format III-A for family and individual needs
Complete format III-B as consolidation capturing all family and individual needs at village level with gap analysis
Prepare comprehensive village development plan and submit to gram sabha
Conduct annual social audit by gram sabha and submit progress reports, utilization certificates, and photographic evidence through online portal

schemes.scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Virasat Ka Samvardhan (PM VIKAS)
benefits: Provides skill development in both traditional crafts and modern sectors to enhance employability of minority youth.
Empowers minority women through leadership and entrepreneurship training, including support to start enterprises.
Offers bridge education for school dropouts to complete classes 8th, 10th, and 12th via open schooling.
Facilitates access to credit, market linkages, and e-commerce platforms for artisans and entrepreneurs.
Promotes preservation and promotion of traditional arts, crafts, and cultural heritage.
Ensures inclusive development by converging with other government schemes and leveraging existing infrastructure.
eligibility: Age should be between 14-45 years for skilling and education component and 18-45 years for Women Leadership and Entrepreneurship component.
33% of total seats in Skilling and Training component, 50% of total seats in Education component and 100% of total seats in Women Leadership and Entrepreneurship component are earmarked for women.

Education qualification for Skilling and Training component would be as per NSQF norms and Education component would be as per NIOS norms. Candidates with aptitude and leadership qualities will be selected for Women Leadership and Entrepreneurship component.
documents_required: Aadhaar card and aadhar linked bank account.
Educational Qualification in line with the training programme being applied for.
application_process: Step 01: Register/Login to SIDH portal to find PM VIKAS scheme and job roles. Use filters such as scheme, mode, and sector to find suitable courses or batches.

How to apply for a training batch? Choose a batch for your preferred job role and submit your interest or application. You may select up to five batches, which will be reviewed by the training partner/training centre.

How to finalize enrolment? After approval from the training partner/training centre, accept the invitation through your portal to confirm enrolment. Track your application status under the 'Applications' section.

schemes.scheme_name: Post Graduate Scholarships for Professional Courses for SC/ST Candidates
schemes.description: The scheme provides financial assistance to SC/ST candidates pursuing postgraduate studies in professional courses like Engineering & Technology, Management, and Pharmacy.
benefits: Scholarship amount varies based on course (₹7800/month for PG courses and ₹4500/month for others).
No contingency payment is required.
Award valid for one academic year.
eligibility: Candidates must belong to SC/ST category.
Should have a graduate degree and admission at postgraduate level in professional subjects from recognized institutions.
Must be pursuing the first year of a postgraduate course during the current academic year.
Cannot change their course of study without approval.
Failure to pass first year or discontinuation due to personal reasons forfeits the scholarship.
application_process: Online application process.
Step 01: Register on National Scholarship Portal.
Step 02: Fill online application form with mandatory details.
Step 03: Verify mobile number via OTP and login.
Step 04: Fill application form and generate application ID/password.
Step 05: Await institution verification by UGC.
For awardees, payment will be processed through Canara Bank branches.
